summary: Matched the ftCommon rebirth animation callback ftCo_Rebirth_Anim. The implementation loads Fighter from Fighter_GObj user_data, calls ftCo_8008A7A8 with fp->ft_data->x24, decrements fp->mv.co.common.x0, and transitions through ftCo_800D5600 when the counter reaches zero. The PR also tightened ftCo_800D5600's header prototype from unknown return/params to void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj). Reported verification was a clean ninja build and 100.0 fuzzy match.
what_changed: Replaced the placeholder /// #ftCo_Rebirth_Anim in src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.c with a matching C implementation.
Implemented ftCo_Rebirth_Anim(Fighter_GObj* gobj) using Fighter* fp = gobj->user_data.
Called ftCo_8008A7A8(gobj, fp->ft_data->x24) before updating the rebirth common state counter.
Decremented fp->mv.co.common.x0 and called ftCo_800D5600(gobj) once that field becomes zero.
Updated the ftCo_800D5600 declaration in src/melee/ft/ft_0D31.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
smart_moves: Used the local call site in ftCo_Rebirth_Anim to prove a concrete ftCo_800D5600 signature instead of leaving the header as UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Kept the callback minimal and ordered exactly as the match required: helper call, counter decrement, zero check, then transition helper.
Reused established fighter callback conventions: Fighter_GObj* parameter, Fighter* from gobj->user_data, and state data under fp->mv.co.common.
decomp_lessons: Small action-state animation callbacks can be matched by identifying the per-frame helper call plus a countdown field in fp->mv.co.common.
When a formerly unknown helper is called directly from a newly matched function, update the header prototype at the same time if the argument and return evidence is clear.
For ftCommon rebirth callbacks, fp->mv.co.common.x0 acts as a countdown in this function; future work should verify nearby rebirth/wait callbacks for the same common-field usage before renaming.
A 100% fuzzy match with a clean ninja build is useful evidence, but this PR includes no reviewer discussion, so broader naming intent beyond the changed symbols is not evidenced.
naming_conventions: Matched public callback name uses the ftCo_Rebirth_Anim pattern for ftCommon action-state animation callbacks.
Related callbacks in the same header follow the suffix convention _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
The helper remains address-style named ftCo_800D5600 even after its prototype was clarified, indicating no semantic name was established in this PR.
Unidentified struct members remained provisional names such as ft_data->x24 and mv.co.common.x0 rather than being over-renamed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Express the countdown as fp->mv.co.common.x0 -= 1 followed by an equality check against zero.
Preserve call order: ftCo_8008A7A8(gobj, fp->ft_data->x24) must occur before decrementing x0 and before the conditional ftCo_800D5600 call.
Use a typed Fighter_GObj* parameter and gobj->user_data load to produce the expected fighter pointer access pattern.
The matching evidence reported in the PR body was fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body states that ninja built clean and the function reached a 100.0 fuzzy match.
follow_up_queries: Audit nearby rebirth callbacks ftCo_Rebirth_IASA, ftCo_Rebirth_Phys, ftCo_Rebirth_Coll, and ftCo_RebirthWait_* for related mv.co.common.x0 semantics.
Look for additional ftCo_800D5600 call sites to confirm whether the void ftCo_800D5600(Fighter_GObj*) prototype is globally sufficient.
Investigate whether fp->ft_data->x24 has a known semantic name used by other ftCommon animation callbacks.
Check whether mv.co.common.x0 can eventually be renamed to a rebirth timer/countdown field once more action states are matched.
